entry.title: SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises - Integration Aspects
entry.content:
SAP published a document that explains integration aspects of SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises, see SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises - Integration Aspects.

This paper provides an overview regarding integration aspects of SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises. The related page in the SAP Help Portal links to this document and provides the context for it – hence, we suggest to first view SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises | SAP Help Portal and subsequently turn to the paper.

The paper addresses cross-product integration aspects, that is, how the products that form SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises integrate with each other and with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
The intention is to not repeat information regarding integration that is available for each of these products, rather, to reference such information assets and put them into context by providing an overview regarding integration aspects across products.

The audiences that we have primarily in mind are enterprise architects and experts in the field who engage with enterprise architects.

This blog is supposed to provide a feedback channel regarding this paper.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Bangalore 2024: FEDML Reference Architecture for Hyperscalers and SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hey Folks, it's Navya again!

Well, as a fellow SAP enthusiast, I know how thrilling it is to dive into the world of SAP learning about the different SAP technologies, collaborating and networking with people and much more. 

Sindhu Gangadharan, SVP & MD SAP Labs IndiaAmongst this, starting this year with our first edition of SAP Inside Track Bangalore 2024, the excitement is palpable, and I'm thrilled to be a part of it. But what makes this event even more special is the opportunity to share my experience and delivering a session in it. So, as we embark on this inaugural journey, let me weave you through the highs, lows, and invaluable lessons learned along the way.

SAP Inside Track events are all about grassroots collaboration and sharing expertise on SAP-related topics.The day kicked off with keynote address by Sindhu Gangadharan, SVP & MD, SAP Labs India and Head User Enablement with her insightful thoughts shedding light on the SAP eco-system and setting the tone for the day, inspiring attendees to engage actively, learn from one another, and leverage their collective expertise to drive innovation and success in the realm of SAP technology.

This year's SIT focused on eight tracks, each offering a wide array of topics spanning SAP Business Technology Platform(BTP), Application Development, Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence & Automation, UI/UX Design, SAP Concur, and S/4 HANA.

Session on FedML with Hyperscaler & SAP Datasphere [In frame speakers: Navya and Lalit]Amidst the diverse array of topics, I had the privilege of presenting the session on "Federated Machine Learning Reference Architecture for Hyperscalers and SAP Datasphere" with my colleague a.ka. mentor @lalitmohan . It was an exhilarating experience to share insights and delve into the intricacies with fellow enthusiasts.

Session on Federated Machine Learning Reference Architecture for Hyperscalers and SAP Datasphere

During the session, we delve to an in-depth exploration of federated machine learning libraries, that applies the Data Federation Architecture of SAP Datasphere for intelligently sourcing SAP as well as non-SAP data for Machine Learning experiments, allowing for model training across distributed data sources while preserving data privacy and security. This innovative architecture enables hyperscalers and organizations leveraging SAP DataSphere to harness the power of machine learning at scale without compromising sensitive data.

But before getting into the usecase front, let us discuss What is FedML? Why FedML?

What is FedML?

Federated Machine Learning is an approach to machine learning where the training data remains distributed over multiple devices or locations, without centrally aggregating it. Instead of bringing all the data to one location, federated learning brings the model to the data or distributes the model across multiple devices or servers. This approach allows for privacy-preserving machine learning, as the raw data remains local and is not shared with a central server or authority.

Why FedML?

  • Data Federation Architecture: FedML leverages SAP Datasphere's Data Federation architecture to intelligently source both SAP and non-SAP data for machine learning experiments.
  • Integration with Hyperscalers: FedML seamlessly integrates with hyperscaler platforms, providing compatibility and scalability for machine learning processes.
  • Abstraction of Data Connection and Load: FedML abstracts the complexities of data connection and loading, simplifying the process of accessing and utilizing data from various sources.
  • Flexible Model Training: FedML offers flexibility in model training by providing support for user-provided training scripts.
  • End-to-End Integration & Minimal Code Requirement: FedML facilitates end-to-end integration of the machine learning pipeline, covering data connection, data loading, model training, deployment, and inferencing stages.

Reference Architecture

After familiarizing ourselves with the fundamentals of integrating FedML with SAP Datasphere, we proceeded to explore two specific use cases:

  • Implementing Databricks FedML with SAP Datasphere

Business Challenge: Large-scale distributed data is crucial for analytics and decision-making. Much of this data is used for predictive modeling and machine learning (ML). With the proliferation of ML platforms, businesses can now efficiently train and deploy models. However, accessing SAP data presents challenges for Data Scientists using Databricks. They depend on data engineers to create pipelines for SAP data extraction and preparation, which is costly and time-consuming. Integrating non-SAP data with SAP data for ML experimentation adds complexity.

Proposed Solution: FedML Databricks library provides functions that enable businesses and data scientists to build, train and deploy machine learning models on ML platforms and eliminates the need for replicating or migrating data out from its original source. By abstracting the data connection, data load, model deployment and model inference on these ML platforms, the FedML Databricks library provides end-to-end integration with just a few lines of code.

In order to try out the solution for this scenerio, you can refer to the Github Repo or refer to this amazing blogpost 

  • Integrating GCP FedML with SAP Datasphere

Business Challenge: In enterprises, for conducting machine learning experiments on hyperscaler platforms, the data often has to be extracted out of source business systems and duplicated in cloud stores of the ML platform. This causes issues such as data inconsistencies and increased TCO resulting from additional data storage costs due to data duplication and expensive data pipelines. Moreover, comparative sales performance analysis on live data is not currently possible with data from cross cloud sources.

Proposed Solution: GCP FedML Library helps you creating an end to end automated solution for ML experiments on hyperscalers(GCP in this case) without moving or duplicating data. This solution uses SAP Datasphere data federation architecture, a unified semantic layer helps model the queries across distributed data sources without the need to extract any data out from anywhere

In order to try out the solution for this scenerio, you can refer to the DC Mission, Github Repo or refer to this amazing blogpost 

In case you want to try out any other Hyperscalar scenerio with FedML and SAP Datasphere, you can refer to the following DC Mission: AWS FedML with Datasphere, Azure FedML with SAP Datasphere

Following our demo on the above scenarios, we concluded with a Q&A session to address any queries, concerns, or feedback from the audience. During the Q&A, participants had the opportunity to delve deeper into the intricacies of federated machine learning and its applications in various industries. 

Conclusion

So there you have it – my whirlwind adventure at SAP Inside Track 24. I am grateful for the opportunity to share my expertise, connect with fellow enthusiasts, and be part of this incredible community-driven event. As I reflect on this experience, I am inspired to continue pushing the boundaries of innovation and making meaningful contributions to the SAP ecosystem. 

SAP Inside Track'24 Team

entry.title: Witness Thomas Volmering Unveil Fusion Development: SAP Build in Action
entry.content:
In the fast-paced world of ERP, innovation isn't just about introducing new features: innovation is about optimizing existing systems while preparing for future demands. A recent webcast led by @Thomas on February 15 delved into this very topic, showcasing the power of SAP Build and SAP Build Code.

However, beyond just demonstrating features, the session provided valuable insights into strategies for maintaining a clean core and extending on SAP BTP.

Clean Core Strategy

The concept of a "Clean Core" was a central theme throughout the webinar. This approach emphasizes keeping the core system as standard and untouched as possible. With a Clean Core strategy, organizations can ensure that their ERP systems remain agile and adaptable to changing business requirements. Upgrades become easier, requiring less manual effort for testing and adapting existing structures.

Streamlined development of extensions and tailored functionalities on SAP BTP with SAP Build simplifies the extension creation process, expediting time-to-market and promoting collaborative efforts.

Fostering Collaboration through Fusion Teams

The concept of collaboration brings Fusion Teams, where individuals with varying expertise collaborate seamlessly toward common goals. Fusion Teams represent a departure from traditional siloed approaches to software development. By bringing together diverse skill sets and perspectives, these teams foster collaboration and creativity, resulting in solutions that are not only technically robust but also closely aligned with business objectives.

A key enabler of collaboration within Fusion Teams is the lobby area of SAP Build – a central hub for fusion development. Here, team members can seamlessly consume components created with SAP Build Code, leveraging the power of pro-code efficiency within the intuitive framework of low-code simplicity. This integration empowers teams to rapidly prototype, iterate, and deploy solutions, accelerating time-to-market and ensuring alignment with business objectives.

Insights for Innovation

If you're ready to unlock the full potential of your ERP system and to drive innovation within your organization, consider utilizing the insights shared in this webinar. Explore SAP Build and SAP Build Code on YouTube to discover how you can maintain a clean core, streamline development, and foster collaboration through Fusion Teams.

To stay updated throughout the year on Application Development and Automation on SAP BTP, regularly browse our public roadmaps for SAP Build + SAP Build Code and… feel free to follow Thomas here on SAP Community and/or on LinkedIn to be notified of his next blog posts!

entry.title: Supporting crypto agility with the BTP Trust Store
entry.content:
You have all heard that change is the new normal. This is true in many ways, including for IT security. Whether it is the cryptographic algorithm, the key length, or an individual key, we must always be ready to change to something “better”, namely more secure. This might not even be due to an attack, but rather be part of continuous efforts to stay ahead of all potential threats.

When it comes to protocols like Transport Layer Security (TLS), the level of security also depends on the root certificates that are the anchor of trust for the TLS-protected communication. In the past, the validity of root certificates could be decades, so a change would be a once in a lifetime thing. Today however, even though the root certificates are not really short-lived, they will still have to be replaced now and then.

Changing the trust anchor of communication in a large landscape requires some preparation, to ensure that everybody can communicate securely and without disruption. If you miss to update the trust configuration across all involved parties, then the change of a root certificate may cause an outage for all scenarios that rely on the trust.

When SAP changes the root certificates that are the trust anchors of BTP, we ensure that all scenarios inside the platform are ready, and the communication is not affected. However, you may also have clients or 3rd party applications that integrate with BTP. For these, the trust is not managed by SAP and so you need to take matters into your own hands.

With the new BTP Trust Store, we want to help you avoid outages by providing information about changes in the trust anchors of SAP BTP early. This gives you the time to roll out new root certificates before they become mandatory.

You will find the required root certificates in several well-known formats in the BTP Trust Store at https://github.com/sap-software/btp-trust-store

The trust store includes 2 sets of root certificates:

The “Required” root certificates are mandatory for all clients or services that communicate via TLS with SAP BTP-based services. Ensure that your clients or services trust these root certificates, and that changes to the list of root certificates are reflected early on your side, such as once per quarter. This will ensure that you can continue to communicate with BTP even if SAP changes root certificates over time.

The “Optional” set of root certificates represents the SAP Global Trust List provided by SAP Global Security, as documented in SAP Note 2801396.

entry.title: Migrate Agent 2 to Agent 3 best practices
entry.content:
Recommendations

Unless you still execute Desktop packages on Intelligent RPA platform, you should migrate all your Agents 2.x to Agents 3.x as it brings many benefits such as easy installation and automatic update (more detail in a blog post that will come soon)

Also you have to know that the Agent 2.x is in maintenance mode, all new features are implemented on Agent 3.x. End of maintenance dates for the Agent 2.0 are:

  • 30/06/2024 for SBPA tenants
  • 30/06/2025 for IRPA tenants

If you have only cloud studio project, you can move from agent 2 to agent 3 really . But we provide some recommendations:

Dev and tests machines:

You have to know that developer machine can have Agent 2 and Agent 3 installed on the same machine in order to make comparative testing. The user will just have to start manually the chosen agent.

Warning : you can’t execute Agent 2 and Agent 3 simultaneously.

Agent 3.x and different SDK versions ensure ascending compatibility, now it's recommended to ensure a test period before deploying on a large scale on production machine.

In order to be sure that your project is compatible with the agent 3, you have to upgrade the  SDK version to the last one. It is with the last version of the SDK that you have to execute your first tests.

There is two different way to procced :

  • Keep same project for both Agent 2 and Agent 3 (recommended)
    • Update SDK to recent version
    • Execute Non regression tests on Agent 2 and Agent 3
  • Diverge project to have separated on Agent 2 and Agent 3 (eg. 1.x for Agent 2, 2.x for Agent 3)
    • Duplicate and update SDK for Agent 3

 

Acceptance machines (a few machines)

After dev tests, you have to be sure that everything is fine in acceptance.

We recommend to follow the manual uninstall/install procedure and keep a certain period of non-regression tests. It’s important to do that on unattended and attended bots.

 

Production machines :

On production machines, Agent 2.x and Agent 3.x should not be installed simultaneously.

Depending of the number of machines and their mode (attended or unattended) you have two ways to do that. It’s explained in the next section.

How to the replace the agent 2 by the agent 3

You have to know that the installation mode is different between the agent 2 and the Agent 3.

The Agent 2 installation was per machine and with admin rights, while Agent 3 installation is per user session without admin rights. It means that one independent agent is installed for every session.

Depending of the number of users/machine that you have to migrate there is two ways :

  • Manual for few users/machines
  • Mass for a lot of users/machines

Manual installation

Prerequisite

    • Requires admin right for Agent 2.X uninstallation.

Steps :

  1. Uninstall Agent 2.X, deploy "Run" registry key for mass installation.
  2. Follow this procedure to install the agent 3.X
  3. The tenant will be configured automatically in the agent 3, but you will have to authenticate agai
  4. You will have to setup the mode (attended or unattended) and load the project if attended.
  5. This procedure has to be done for every user on the same machine.

Mass installation

Prerequisite

    • Requires admin right for Agent 2.X uninstallation.
    • IT/Admin rights to configure Agent 3.X installation files

Steps

  1. Uninstall Agent 2
  2. Follow this procedure the agent 3.X will be installed during session start
  3. The tenant will be configured automatically in the agent 3, but you will have to authenticate agai
  4. You will have to setup the mode (attended or unattended) and load the project if attended.

The service

The service is optional with Agent 3, it is only required in unattended mode for production machine, as it allows to automate opening session at boot time, and unlock user session when required (keystroke actions, ...)

The service was installed with the agent 2, it’s not the case with the Agent 3 because of the per user installation mode (you will need admin right). To install the service follow this .

entry.title: SAP Recognized as a Leader in 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for iPaaS
entry.content:
We are proud to announce that Gartner has recognized SAP as a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for Integration Platform as a Service (iPaaS) worldwide. SAP’s Integration Suite was evaluated and based on various factors including reviews of current users of our service. We were recognized as a Leader for the fourth consecutive time. We believe we have received this recognition for the targeted efforts to deliver a customer-centric service with a strong focus on innovation and being future-ready.

Built as a single, comprehensive integration platform, SAP Integration Suite empowers you to integrate and automate processes with speed and confidence across SAP and third-party landscapes. You can leverage our pre-built integrations, APIs, connectors and best practices to design end-to-end automated business processes and deliver innovations that can set you apart from your competition. You can learn more about it here.

SAP is recognized for its ability to execute, we believe this validates our customer-centric approach to product development and evolution. Our innovations which include new integration patterns and artificial intelligence-based features that enhance developer productivity, were rated highly.

We offer a strong commercial and product support presence across all geographical regions. This makes Integration Suite one of the few truly global managed iPaaS offerings suitable for all market segments. We also offer deployment flexibility with our service’s ability to be deployed on client data centers, data centers for regulated industries and private cloud regions.

It gives us immense satisfaction to see that 97% of our customers strongly recommend SAP Integration Suite on Gartner® Peer Insights™. We feel this is an indicator of our customer-focused execution and a shot in the arm for our teams to double down on our roadmap and continue to deliver innovations. Customers also reported that their trust in our roadmap and vision is 4.5% higher than the market average reported by We focus on ensuring that our customers have an enterprise-grade feature set that can help them remain competitive in a market that continues to evolve at a rapid pace, while also ensuring that their investments in SAP Integration Suite is protected for the foreseeable future.

What makes Integration Suite a Leader in the iPaaS Market?

SAP Integration Suite is an AI-assisted integration platform as a service (iPaaS). With this single solution, you can connect applications from SAP and third parties, whether they run on-premise or across clouds. You can use a combination of integration modes to address specific use cases and run your integrations on major public clouds for greater flexibility.

Robust functionality in SAP Integration Suite means you can confidently integrate and manage your mission-critical processes across SAP and third-party applications in complex and distributed landscapes.

Here are some of the key value propositions that we believe set SAP Integration Suite apart from the competition:

  1. Third-Party Connectivity: SAP Integration Suite is a general-purpose iPaaS that can easily integrate with third-party applications as well. We offer 250+ third-party application connectors in addition to protocol-based connectors that can be used to connect with different systems. We also have a strong roadmap to increase the number of non-SAP connectors, further demonstrating our intent to be an excellent all-purpose iPaaS.
  2. Runtime Deployment Flexibility: Depending on your needs and security constraints, you can choose to deploy our runtime in the public cloud, private cloud, within your private data centres, or employ a hybrid setup with runtimes in both public and private regions.
  3. Power of Artificial Intelligence: We at SAP are committed to leveraging artificial intelligence in the Integration Suite to boost developer productivity and insights via behavioural patterns that include mapping proposals, industry-based inter selection and scenario generation. This is a priority topic for us and we are investing continuously to offer a true next-gen integration platform.
  4. Enterprise Automation: We at SAP believe that Process Automation is the key to any digital transformation and our endeavour is to equip you with the tools necessary to build end-to-end intelligent business and IT processes. In combination with SAP Signavio and SAP Build, we offer the perfect enterprise trifecta for Enterprise Automation. 
  5. API and Event-Driven Integration: Support for modern integration patterns is a critical aspect of any future-proof iPaaS. Integration Suite supports and offers best practices for both API-centric and event-driven integrations. We offer out-of-the-box 600+ business events and ~4000 APIs on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, with the number growing at a rapid pace as more SAP LoBs publish their content on the hub. 
  6. SAP Process Orchestration Move: We are very cognizant of the fact that many of our customers have significant investments in SAP Process Orchestration, and we are fully committed to helping them transition to Integration Suite with the least business disruption and maximum reusability while staying true to the digital transformation objective. Our migration tools and special programs like Migration Factory focused on migration help customers craft a strategic journey that will ensure the lowest TCO and highest value in their transition from the on-premise SAP Process Orchestration to the hybrid SAP Integration Suite.
  7. Business Integration Content: Getting started quickly by using integration packages infused with business expertise and best-in-class industry experience on the SAP Business Accelerator hub has been one of our unique offerings for our customers. We engage our vibrant community to develop content in a collaborative model and offer it to our customer base. As of today, SAP offers 3400+ pre-packaged Integrations including SAP and third-party integration scenarios.

 Summary

We are very grateful for the vote of confidence from our customers, partners, and analysts that has culminated in SAP. We are happy to be recognized as a Leader in Gartner Magic Quadrant for Enterprise iPaaS. We feel, this is validation and it has boosted our confidence that we are heading in the right direction and will further energize us to deliver more customer-centric features and innovations while staying true to our core principles of high developer productivity, happy user experience, and optimized total cost of ownership (TCO).

 Where Can I Learn More?  

  • Read the News Center feature by JG Chirapurath
  • Access the full report 
  • SAP Integration Suite Product Page
  • Explore exclusive iPaaS content on SAP Integration Space
  • Integration Suite @ Discovery Center
  • OpenSAP Course on SAP Integration Suite  
  • Manage B2B Scenarios Effectively with SAP Integration Suite | openSAP
  • SAP Integration Suite Community  
  • SAP Integration Suite on SAP Help Portal  
GARTNER is a registered trademark and service mark of Gartner and Magic Quadrant and Peer Insights are a registered trademark, of Gartner, Inc. and/or its affiliates in the U.S. and internationally and are used herein with permission. All rights reserved.
Gartner Peer Insights content consists of the opinions of individual end-users based on their own experiences with the vendors listed on the platform, should not be construed as statements of fact, nor do they represent the views of Gartner or its affiliates. Gartner does not endorse any vendor, product or service depicted in this content nor makes any warranties, expressed or implied, with respect to this content, about its accuracy or completeness, including any warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose.
Gartner does not endorse any vendor, product or service depicted in its research publications and does not advise technology users to select only those vendors with the highest ratings or other designation. Gartner research publications consist of the opinions of Gartner’s research organization and should not be construed as statements of fact. Gartner disclaims all warranties, expressed or implied, with respect to this research, including any warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose.
This graphic was published by Gartner, Inc. as part of a larger research document and should be evaluated in the context of the entire document. The Gartner document is available upon request from SAP.

entry.title: SAP BTP Innobytes – February 2024
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform brings together application development, automation, data and analytics, integration, and AI capabilities into one unified environment optimized for SAP applications. Learn more: sap.com/btp

As we bid farewell to February, we welcome yet another exciting release of SAP BTP Innobytes! With each passing month comes a fresh wave of innovations, and this February is no exception. This release unveils highlights from three areas – Automation, Data and Analytics, and Application Development. Let's dive in!

Data & Analytics Innovations on SAP BTP

SAP Analytics Cloud: Just Ask

First up we are introducing SAP Analytics Cloud's Just Ask feature, powered by next-generation natural language query capabilities. Imagine being able to access and query your data models simply by asking questions in plain language. With Just Ask, it's not only possible, it's intuitive. Whether you're querying from the top menu or the home screen, Just Ask understands context, time, and even synonyms, making data analysis a breeze.

To learn more about SAP Analytics Cloud: Just Ask, check out:

  • Generative AI for Analytics & Planning
  • Preview Demo Video
  • Initial Release of SAP Analytics Cloud: Just Ask, SAP Community Blog Post

Topic Experts: @Orla_C and @francois_imberton 

Application Development Innovations on SAP BTP

Transport of SAP Build Process Automation Content Using SAP Cloud Transport Management

Next, we are unveiling SAP Cloud Transport Management (cTMS), the answer to many customers' need for a unified approach to content transport within SAP BTP. With cTMS, promoting projects between tenants is seamless, with integration into change management tools like Cloud ALM or Solution Manager.

After the setup has been completed, you can promote projects from the lobby. This creates a transport request in cTMS which can be handled like any other content.

The next step is to deploy the content to the next tenant on the transport route, in this screenshot, the QA environment.


In the QA tenant's lobby, the project appears as importable content and can now be activated.


The integration with cTMS also enables SAP Build Process Automation content to be handled with the change management tools in Cloud ALM or Solution Manager.

To learn more, check out:

  • SAP Build Process Automation – Promoting Projects

Topic Expert: @Harald_Stevens 

Automation Innovations on SAP BTP

SAP Build Process Automation: Vertical Process Editor

Last, but certainly not least, SAP Build Process Automation's Vertical Process Editor. The newly released process editor gives you an instant top-to-bottom overview of even the most complex process models. With enhanced drag-and-drop capabilities and a simplified go-to-step feature, editing and rearranging steps have never been easier.

Drag and DropGo-To-Step

Say goodbye to tedious process design and hello to streamlined efficiency!

For more information, check out:

  • Layout and Usability Improvements in the Process Editor

Topic Expert: @JesseSukhbayar

Share Your Thoughts

We would love to hear what you think about the series. How do you like the video? Are there any SAP BTP topics that you’d like us to share more about? Leave your comments down below and we’re happy to connect with you.

Thank you for your support and happy viewing, sharing, and enjoying!

If you like SAP BTP Innobytes, you may also be interested in:

  • SAP BTP Ask Me Anything: monthly LIVE Q&A series with hot topics and product experts
  • SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos
  • SAP BTP Talk Podcast: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP
  • What's New with SAP BTP: quarterly LIVE series for SAP BTP innovation highlights, with demos and time for Q&A

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence meets SAP Customer Engagement Initiative - Register until March 08, 2024!
entry.content:
It has never been a more exciting time for AI! Generative AI and large language models (LLMs) are dominating headlines and making AI a part of every business conversation.

But what is generative AI?

Generative AI, a form of artificial intelligence, can produce text, images, and varied content based on its training data. At SAP, we integrate this technology with extensive industry-specific data and deep process knowledge to create innovative AI capabilities for the SAP applications you use every day. It’s built-in, relevant to your business, and responsible by design.

The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner, to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions. By that we can create solutions that better meet your and other customers’ needs, leveraging process and technology innovations.

Seize this opportunity to participate in our projects with an AI flavor! Register until March 08, 2024:

  • AI Business Agents with SAP BTP: An Exploration Project
  • AI & GenAi Integration with Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management
  • Business AI in Asset Performance Management
  • Create your extensions with SAP Build and SAP Build Code
  • GenAI for Situation Handling
  • Hybrid integration with SAP Integration Suite
  • Intelligent Closing in the Public Cloud ERP
  • Leveraging Generative AI capabilities in Business Partner Master data
  • Smart Data Load - AI Supported Chart of Accounts Mapping 

Visit the Customer Influence website on influence.sap.com for further information.

Interested in other topics as well? Find here the list of all projects.

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

Thank you in advance for your participation,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

entry.title: Do you Feel the Pressure to Innovate and Embrace Cutting-Edge Technologies like GenAI?
entry.content:
Navigating the complex landscape of innovation can be daunting. It's not just about embracing the latest trends; it's about ensuring that innovation aligns with the needs of your people, is economically viable, and technically feasible. Adding AI into the mix further complicates the equation, as the possibilities it offers are both vast and sometimes overwhelming.

You might find yourself asking, "How can I find the right balance between these factors, including the integration of AI, to create tangible value?"

While there are numerous approaches to this question, our stand-out solution is:

SAP AppHaus’ Human-Centred Approach to Innovation, coupled with its Generative AI Explore Workshop.

Why focus on human-centred design? Often, the most effective way to improve processes is to start by understanding the needs and pain points of the people who actually perform the work. While it may seem intuitive to prioritize certain aspects of innovation, it's only through engaging with end-users that you can truly identify where innovation is most needed.

But where to begin? How do other people and organizations tackle this innovation pressure? This is where thought leaders come into play—individuals who possess the expertise, insights, and innovative ideas to guide you in the right direction.

On March 13, 2024, you’ll have the opportunity to hear from Andreas Hauser, Senior Vice President and Head of the SAP AppHaus Network. With a wealth of experience in orchestrating innovation projects worldwide run by the global SAP AppHaus Network teams, Andreas collaborates closely with experts across diverse fields, gaining insights which he uses to enrich projects with customers.

Join and listen to Andreas share his invaluable insights, and actively engage in dialogue. Post your questions, share your perspectives, and glean insights from a diverse community of innovators.

By the end of the session, you'll emerge with a deeper understanding of today's opportunities, armed with strategies for leveraging SAP's Business AI with SAP Business Technology Platform, in conjunction with the SAP AppHaus Network to propel your innovation journey forward. You will get actionable insights, practical tips, and exclusive access to valuable resources that will empower you to navigate the ever-evolving landscape of innovation with confidence.

Join us here: https://www.youtube.com/live/D_QVQviszaM?si=R0un6RZCOWI-3R5M

Add to calendar: https://url.sap/uxogi7 

We look forward to seeing you!!

entry.title: #SITBLR 2024: Implementing end-to-end use cases with SAP BTP and Hyperscalers
entry.content:
Still buzzing from the incredible experience at SAP Inside Track 2024! Taking the stage as a session speaker alongside my colleague @uma-anbazhagan was an absolute thrill ! The journey from preparing my presentation to delivering it in front of fellow enthusiasts was nothing short of exhilarating. In this blog post, I'm excited to share the highlights of my experience, from the technical insights shared during our session to the candid moments that made it a truly unforgettable occasion.
 
SAP Inside Track events serve as platforms for collaborative knowledge exchange within the SAP ecosystem. The day commenced with a keynote address by Sindhu Gangadharan, SVP & MD, SAP Labs India and Head User Enablement, whose insights illuminated the intricacies of the SAP ecosystem, setting a tone of inspiration for attendees. The emphasis was on active engagement, mutual learning, and leveraging collective expertise to drive innovation and success in SAP technology. 

This year's SAP Inside Track featured eight tracks, each covering a wide range of topics including SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), Application Development, Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence & Automation, UI/UX Design, SAP Concur, and SAP S/4 HANA, providing attendees with a comprehensive exploration of key areas within the SAP landscape.  


Session on "Implementing end-to-end use cases with SAP BTP and Hyperscalers" 


Our session focused on showcasing some of the joint reference architectures developed by SAP and Hyperscalers that organizations can harness to drive innovation, scalability and efficiency in their business operations. 

In Frame Speakers: Swati and Uma

The session started with SAP’s multi-cloud strategy and the guiding principles and directions to get started with building interoperable SAP business applications, followed by the end-to-end use-cases developed as part of Joint-Reference Architecture collaboration of SAP and Hyperscalers like AWS, GCP and Microsoft Azure. We then delved into pivotal pillars such as Platform Foundation, Application Development, Integration, Data and Analytics, and Artificial Intelligence and talked about architecture in each of these pillars. 

Each of the reference architecture that was talked about leveraged complimenting services from both the platforms and gave an insight into how SAP and Hyperscalers are working together to achieve the best of both worlds. 

 

 

You can implement these scenarios yourself by following the step-by-step documentation provided for each of these use-cases built on SAP Discovery Center. Scan the QR code to try out the scenario now! 

End-to-end use cases in SAP Discovery Center

 

Conclusion: 

As I come to the end of my journey at SAP Inside Track 2024, I find myself in awe of the journey from being a volunteer last year to standing as a session speaker this year. It's been an exhilarating ride, marked by growth, collaboration, and countless moments of inspiration. Alongside my colleagues, who delivered exceptional sessions across various tracks, I've had the privilege of playing a part in this vibrant event. Reflecting on these experiences, I'm struck by the profound impact of community-driven initiatives like SAP Inside Track in fostering collaboration and driving innovation. Looking forward to contributing to more such events in the future! 😊

There are upcoming SAP Inside Track events getting planned, do join us and share your experiences and expertise by following SAP Community

 

Team PAA at #sitBLR'24

entry.title: Set up SAP Generative AI Hub in an SAP BTP Enterprise Account and Consume Generative AI Model
entry.content:
SAP Generative AI Hub is now available with the SAP AI Core extended service plan. By leveraging Generative AI Hub, SAP partners and customers can build AI-powered extensions and apps on SAP BTP.
 
As part of the SAP AI Core, Generative AI Hub provides access to the most common large language models (LLMs) from different providers. To integrate an LLM via Generative AI Hub into an application, there are a few important steps to follow, that will be covered in this blog:

  • Create an SAP AI Core service instance.
  • Create an LLM configuration to reference a model provider specific executable.
  • Use the configuration to create a deployment.
    The configuration and deployment can be created either via the SAP AI Launchpad UI or using the SAP AI Core APIs.
  • For the created deployment, SAP AI Core provides a unique URL that can be used to access the LLM. Use this URL to infuse generative AI features into your application. Embedding the use of the URL into an application is not covered in this blog. Instead, the use of the deployment URL is demonstrated with Postman.

Prerequisites

  • You have an SAP BTP Enterprise Account.
  • You have an entitlement for SAP AI Core, Service Plan "Extended".
  • You have an entitlement for SAP AI Launchpad, Service Plan "Standard".
  • You have an entitlement and free quota for SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime.

SAP AI Core Setup

First, make sure with your global account administrator that all services listed in the Prerequisites section have service plan quota assigned to your global account.

As subaccount administrator, go to the account level, Entitlements -> Entity Assignments screen and select your subaccount. Click Edit, then Add Service Plans to configure entitlement.

Add 1 unit quota for the extended service plan then save entitlements update.

Create SAP AI Core Service Instance

Navigate to your subaccount and select Services -> Instances and Subscriptions. Click on Create. Fill out the fields as shown below and click Create.

In case you have an existing standard plan service instance, the creation will fail as shown below.

Update existing SAP AI Core service instance instead. For that, go back to Services -> Instances and Subscriptions and search for the SAP AI Core standard service plan service instance. Click on Update button illustrated below:

In the next screen, select extended plan instead of the standard plan and Update Instance.

Create Service Key

On the Instances and Subscriptions screen, find your SAP AI Core service instance and choose Create Service Key from the three dots dropdown.

Enter the name and click Create.

Download and store your service key json file locally. You will use it in the next steps.

 

SAP AI Launchpad Setup

As subaccount administrator, go to the account level, Entitlements -> Entity Assignments screen and select your subaccount. Click Edit, then Add Service Plans to configure entitlement.

Add 1 unit quota for the standard service plan then save entitlements update.

Subscribe to SAP AI Launchpad

SAP AI Launchpad is a SaaS application, and you need to subscribe to it. Follow these SAP Help steps to create a subscription: https://help.sap.com/docs/ai-launchpad/sap-ai-launchpad/subscribe-to-sap-ai-launchpad

Set up User Access to SAP AI Launchpad

In your subaccount go to Security -> Users and select your user. Assign role collections as required. Follow these SAP Help steps: https://help.sap.com/docs/ai-launchpad/sap-ai-launchpad/allow-access-to-sap-ai-launchpad

If you want access to Generative AI Hub features, don’t assign role collections containing “without_genai”. If you want to be editor for specific features, assign only the editor role collections, and not both, viewer and editor role collections for that feature.

 

Create Connection between SAP AI Launchpad and SAP AI Core

Following subscription, you’ll be able to launch SAP AI Launchpad application. Go to Instances and Subscriptions screen in your subaccount. You’ll see your SAP AI Launchpad application under the Subscriptions. Click on it to open it.

Workspaces app is used to manage connections between SAP AI Launchpad and AI runtimes such as SAP AI Core. AI runtimes provide resources to process AI activities such as training and inference.

In the Workspaces, you need to add a new AI API connection. Click on Add and fill out the fields as shown below. Under the Service Key field, click the upload symbol to upload the SAP AI Core service key json file that you stored locally as per Create Service Key section.

Click on Create and the connection will be established as shown below.

You also need to select a resource group so that you can perform ML Operations tasks. Resource groups represent a virtual collection of related resources within the scope of your AI runtime. Runtime entities such as executions, deployments, configurations, and artifacts belong to a specific resource group and are not shared across resource groups.

During subscription to SAP AI Launchpad, when your tenant is onboarded, a default resource group is automatically created. For your connection, you can select the default resource group as shown in the picture above.

 

Create a Configuration

To be able to deploy and consume LLMs you first need to create a configuration that will reference a model provider specific executable.

An executable is used to define training or serving pipelines for an AI use case. An executable that is used to serve (deploy) an AI model is called a serving executable.

Access to the LLMs is provided under the global AI scenario foundation-models, which is managed by SAP AI Core. Individual models are provided as executables in the form of serving templates and accessed by choosing the corresponding template for the desired model.

See models that are currently supported here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/models-and-scenarios-in-generative-ai-hub

In SAP AI Launchpad, go to ML Operations -> Configurations and click Create.

Give your configuration a name, select the foundation-models scenario and the version, and select the serving executable.

Click Next to enter Input Parameters.

Select model name you want to deploy. See model version in the link provided above. Note that the model version can be given as latest. If the model version is not specified, it will be set to latest by default.

Click on Next -> Review -> Create to create the configuration.

When you now click on ML Operations -> Configurations on the left-hand side menu, you’ll see the created configuration.

 

Create a Deployment

A deployment runs a model for serving (inferencing) purposes.

A serving executable is an AI pipeline that serves or deploys a model for online predictions. As we saw in the previous section, you use a configuration to specify values for the serving executable, such as the model as an input artifact. The configuration is then used to create a deployment.

A running deployment can only reference one configuration. Multiple deployments can be created using the same configuration, resulting in separate endpoints for online predictions.

You use SAP AI Launchpad to create deployments for your runtime connection. Deployments that are implemented on an SAP AI Core runtime produce HTTPS endpoints. These endpoints can be used to access the LLM.

Following the previous step, click on the created configuration, then on Create Deployment.

Keep Duration as Standard and click on Review then Create to create the deployment.

Once the deployment created, wait for the current status to be set to RUNNING.

It could take a few minutes for the deployment to go from UNKNOWN status to RUNNING status.

Take note of the deployment URL as you will use it in the next section.

Please note that both, the configuration and the deployment can also be created by using SAP AI Core APIs. See the API Reference on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub https://api.sap.com/api/AI_CORE_API/resource/Configuration,

and https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/create-deployment-for-generative-ai-model-in-sap-ai-core#using-the-api for details. Use the AI_API_URL from your SAP AI Core service key as the "apiurl".

Consume Generative AI Models

Now that you have a deployment running, you can run inference requests against it, and it will return the results from the LLM. LLMs have broad applicability for a range of natural language processing tasks, including text prediction and generation, content summary, translation, content classification and categorization, conversational AI and chatbots, etc.

Models from Azure OpenAI are accessed through a private instance of the chat-completion API.
The endpoint to use is {{deploymentUrl}}/chat/completions?api-version={{api-version}}.
For more info on this API and version to use, see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference#chat-completions

With each inference request, you need to pass SAP AI Core access token and AI-Resource-Group key value in the header. To get a new token, use OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant type and find client ID, client secret and access token URL values in your SAP AI Core service key. See pictures below:

 

The query is sent in the body of the request, under “messages”. There are other optional parameters that you can include, and for their definitions, see https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/consume-generative-ai-models-using-sap-ai-core

The picture below shows the body and response for a text summarizing request. For more examples and details, see the same link above.

 

 

In conclusion, all the steps covered in this blog are prerequisites to integrating an LLM via SAP Generative AI Hub into extensions and applications built on SAP BTP, with the purpose of adding generative AI features and functionalities to your application. Embedding the use of deployment URL programmatically into your application can be at the basis of building such functionalities.

 

 

If you are a partner that needs structured support in building your solutions on SAP BTP, check out our Co-Innovated with SAP service: Co-Innovated with SAP Service | Innovation | Partner Benefits Catalog | Partner Marketplace

If you need additional details, reach out to us at coinnovate@sap.com.

entry.title: SAP Business Technology Platform: “Change Agent” or “Scale Agent” Part 2 – BTP Strategy
entry.content:
The importance of having an SAP BTP strategy has increased in recent years as organizations face rapidly changing technologies and digital transformation requirements. These challenges necessitate having a different strategy to inject innovation into their transformation journey, as opposed to their Business as Usual (BAU) IT.

 

The broad goals of SAP BTP are to enable Innovation which fuels the “Digital First” approach, and is required for organizations to differentiate from their competition constantly. Secondly, it provides the necessary technologies to improve their products and services. Finally, it offers insights from existing processes, suggests improvements using the latest technologies with agility and adaptability, good examples being Business AI, Generative AI & Automation services.

 

What parameters drive innovation ?

Customers need to consider several parameters that require them to approach innovation differently. Here are a few ways how organizations can introduce innovation into their businesses:

 

·      Nimble and agile approach

One needs to be creative and think on their feet to survive. Embracing a startup mindset and finding new ways to grow allows an organization to pivot and make changes quickly.

 

·      Willingness to accept failure of an idea

Taking risks and trying new things can push the organization out of its comfort zone. Encouraging trial of new ideas in an agile way is important, but if an idea fails, the team should be motivated to move on to the next one.

 

·      Timeliness in decision making

Rapid decision-making is crucial, and individuals within the organization need to think like entrepreneurs and act quickly. This gives the organization an advantage when changes occur and decisions need to be made promptly.

 

·      Encouraging creative ideas

Encouraging creative ideas Every innovative strategy springs from the constraints of resources. Smaller teams often find creative solutions to problems, leading to new products and processes. Employing innovative techniques like design thinking can solve even complex problems using creative ideas.

 

·      Embracing Collaboration

The collective talents and skills of the team play a crucial role in innovation as each team member has unique ideas. By creating a culture of trust, respect, and collaboration within the team, an environment friendly to innovative ideas can be nurtured.

 

In addition, the choice of platform, opting for best-of-suite vs best-of-breed approach, best practices and methodology, governance models or lack thereof, availability of skills including reskilling, engagement models with business, agile development, etc., need to be considered.

 

BTP Usage Archetypes

SAP BTP is used in various ways. Primarily, it is used to keep the “Core Clean” for SAP ERP, which means most of the customizations should be done in SAP BTP. SAP BTP is also used as an extension platform along with each SAP Application (for example: Success factors, Ariba, etc.), and thirdly, SAP BTP is used to bring in new technologies like AI & Generative AI, Automation, SAP Build to introduce new capabilities within the organization's business processes

 

Three ways of using SAP BTP are categorized as archetypes in Figure 1:

1.     Application Modernization

2.     Data with Value

 

Figure 1 – Three archetypes and three levels of usage

 

Application Modernization

The first part of the solution involves evaluating and remediating custom code objects that are part of older ERP systems. The goal is to identify which code is obsolete and which code needs to be updated or redesigned. Some of the custom code can be easily migrated to SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) with minimal effort, especially if it is written in ABAP. However, some code may require a more thorough rethinking and redesign to be integrated as side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP.

 

The second part of the solution involves developing custom standalone applications that may require re-platforming. This could include building portals for vendors, suppliers, or customers, as well as developing mobile applications for various purposes such as approvals, inspections, warehouse management, logistics, and sales. These applications need to integrate with SAP Applications for accessing and updating master data. In some cases, integration with non-SAP applications may also be required. Overall, the goal is to extend the functionality of SAP ERP while keeping the core system clean and leveraging the capabilities of SAP BTP for side-by-side extensions and integration with other applications.

 

Data with Value

Managing and leveraging data in today's age can be quite challenging. The increasing demand for enterprise-wide data and the presence of fragmented data sources and catalogs make it difficult to have a comprehensive view of data and its lineage.

 

The diverse nature of data sources, including applications, cloud, and on-premises systems, often results in the need for accessing data from different technology stacks. This constant challenge of accessing and integrating data from various sources can impact data management costs and efficiency, especially when dealing with multi-petabyte data lakes where replicating data becomes impractical.

 

Once the data challenge is addressed, organizations can focus on extracting valuable insights from their data. This involves performing data transformations, analysis, and deriving meaningful information to drive decision-making and gain a competitive advantage.

 

AI, Generative AI & Automation

Artificial intelligence (AI) and Generative AI have indeed gained significant attention and are no longer just hype. They have become a reality and are being applied in various industries. One crucial aspect of leveraging AI effectively is combining it with business-specific data. By integrating AI or Generative AI with business data, organizations can address complex data that often resides in multiple systems. Failing to include business data in AI processes can lead to the loss of valuable insights that could be extracted and combined.

 

As we move forward, the field of business AI will continue to mature. It is expected that companies will increasingly utilize Large Language Models (LLMs) for a wide range of tasks. Organizations will likely work with trusted technology providers who employ the most suitable LLM for specific tasks, optimizing accuracy while considering the computational cost trade-off. These tasks may include content creation, code creation, question answering, or summarization.

 

Business leaders will seek AI solutions that go beyond industry buzz and deliver practical use cases in real-life scenarios. They will also recognize that the effectiveness of AI is heavily dependent on the quality and availability of data. With SAP systems processing a vast amount of data and transactions, SAP is uniquely positioned to assist organizations in accessing, visualizing, and taking decisive action based on this data, ultimately driving their success.

 

BTP COE Organizational model

Though innovation is usually perceived as IT-driven, what's truly needed is for business units within an organization to embrace and incorporate these latest technologies to enhance every business process.

 

Bridge to Innovation

To achieve this, organizations need a "bridge" between business and technology. Organizations require a "niche team" that differs from the traditional IT teams. This team provides both technological know-how and bridges business requirements through solutions driven by innovative services. They offer expertise, guidance, and best practices aimed at fostering innovation, standardization, and knowledge sharing across the organization. In simple terms, this team introduces an "Innovation Mind-set" to the organization. A representation of BTP COE's position is provided in Figure 2 below.

 

Figure 2 – Bridge to Innovation

 

Some of the key elements that this team can introduce include a "CLOUD FIRST", "AGILE", and "ADAPTABLE" approach. Along with these, they can bring in best practices, an effective engagement model with the business, and essential technology skills, enabling them to respond swiftly to business needs.

 

The current technological trends include AI, Generative AI, and automation. This team not only understands these but can also translate business requirements to utilize these cutting-edge technologies within the cloud paradigm. Several advantages can be realized using the cloud (SAP BTP), such as reducing "time" by building quick prototypes to validate with the business before investing more time in building pilots. This approach also aligns with the concept of "Fail Fast"; if a technology does not meet the business requirements, they can quickly move on to the next solution.

Customer BTP COE

To assist our customers in bringing innovation into their business processes, SAP offers the opportunity to establish a Customer BTP Center of Excellence (Customer BTP COE). The Customer BTP COE is a core team of experts possessing BTP skills and knowledge and uses innovative services to build applications that enhance the capabilities of their SAP applications. It serves as the driver for continuous success to maximize return on investment.

 

Following a proven methodology, SAP employs the Customer BTP COE Management framework, which can serve as a guide for establishing your own Customer BTP COE.

 

Customer BTP COE Framework

The objective of the Customer BTP COE Framework is to assist organizations in establishing a BTP COE within their organization, taking into account certain pre-requisites. This section provides a holistic overview of the Customer BTP COE Management framework, elaborating on the interdependencies between the different layers, while offering insights into areas such as BTP Strategy, BTP Architecture & Services, BTP COE Governance and Organization, Processes & Tools, and People & Skills. With this framework, you'll gain a better understanding of the initial parameters required for the establishment of a successful Customer BTP COE.

 

Figure 3 – BTP COE Framework

 

The paragraph below guides customers through the Customer BTP COE framework (Figure 3) and its various sections:

·      Customer BTP Strategy

·      BTP Architecture & Services

·      Organization and Governance

·      Processes and Tools

·      People and skills

 

The "BTP Strategy" section explains how innovation is implemented to improve business processes and generate tangible business value. The "BTP Architecture & Services" layer refers to building a BTP architecture based on archetype chosen.

 

The "Organization & Governance" section provides the necessary structure for introducing the latest technology to address gaps in business processes, improve these processes, and glean meaningful insights from data across the organization. "Processes and Tools" describes how the Customer BTP COE operates and delivers its services to the business.

 

Lastly, the "People and Skills" dimension refers to the design of the overall Customer COE organization and how the roles, skills, and staff are aligned to meet business objectives. For a business driven by innovation, it is crucial to establish and manage the entire Customer BTP COE structure holistically, as various functions are interconnected..

 

Conclusion

We conclude this section by elucidating the role of SAP BTP in an organization, and how the Customer BTP COE team can assist in achieving an organization's innovation goals. We also discussed the broad BTP usage archetypes based on customer needs. The Customer BTP COE framework is designed to incorporate critical elements through best practices.

 

In our next blog, we will delve deep into the BTP and BTP COE topics. See you soon!

#SAPBTP hashtag
#enterprisecloud hashtag
#SAPBTPCOE hashtag
#partnership hashtag
#platformadvisory

@Anil bhaskaran @Peter moore @Manjunath chandrashekar, @Joni Wong Angkasa, @Amandeep kaur budhiraja

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter March 2024
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

Please note: A one-time registration for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support [accessible here] is necessary to register for these events.

How to Migrate Business Data into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit

Would you like to learn more about how your legacy data can be migrated to the new SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system? Join our upcoming Meet-the-Expert session on April 10 and get an overview on the basic features and functions of the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit.
Details in this blog post

Get started with the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Are you new to the test automation tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition or keen to learn more about the embedded features and integrations available? We’ve scheduled two Meet-the-Expert sessions in March to help you get started.
Register through the blog post: Get started with the test automation tool

Release Navigator for SAP BTP: What's Next in 2024

Easier access, simplified home page UX, translation feature access - read all about what's new for the Release Navigator for SAP BTP since Q4 2023 and what's coming in 2024. The release navigator consolidates essential release content from all across the SAP ecosystem. Accessing release content has never been easier.
Read the blog and register for the session on March 7

 

New Continuous Quality Check (CQC) for Deployment Readiness for SAP Customer Data Cloud

We’ll perform a check of your configuration to help avoid any issues with your go-live, or do a health check for you, as part of your support entitlement, with no additional fee. Curious to learn more about this amazing tool?
Dive deeper into its extensive benefits and features in our full-length article and ask questions to the CX value map team.

 

E-Learning series: SAP Datasphere Connectivity

We’re excited to announce our brand new E-Learnings available to help guide you through some key connectivity topics with SAP Datasphere. This series focuses on bidirectional integration between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud, and configuring cloud connector and data provisioning agent when setting up connectivity to on premise systems.
Enroll the series: SAP Datasphere connectivity

 

New dates scheduled: Plan your Conversion to SAP Datasphere with BW Bridge, or SAP BW/4HANA

Due to popular demand we have added new session dates in March for our Expert-guided Implementation: Plan your Conversion to SAP Datasphere with BW Bridge, or SAP BW/4HANA. During this multiday virtual classroom session, you’ll receive guidance and training on SAP BW/4HANA, SAP Datasphere, and the conversion strategies associated with these SAP data warehousing solutions.
Register today: Plan your conversion to SAP Datasphere

 

Explore SAP’s Clean Core Approach and Dive Deep Into Extensibility

In this blog, you find information about the role of extensibility concepts and practices that supports the Clean Core model thus increase your business efficiency and reduce maintenance.
Further details (value maps access required)

 

SAP Cloud ALM for service – Meet-the-Expert Session

Discover how SAP is innovating the Service Delivery process by leveraging the capabilities of SAP Cloud Technology and providing new functionalities via SAP Cloud ALM.
Further details in this blog post: SAP Cloud ALM for service

 

Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Finance – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about all phases and activities involved in Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA. We have two sessions in March, available in English and Chinese language.

More in this blog post

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 Early Release Series

We hosted a series of 19 live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Missed the live sessions? No problem. Take advantage of the entire series or choose the sessions most relevant to you.
Click here to access the replays

 

Migration to SAP Cloud Identity Services: Open Office Hours for Customers and Partners

SAP is hosting Open Office Hours for customers and partners who need support to migrate to SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication service and/or Identity Provisioning service). SAP Cloud Identity Services are the best practice for a secure user login experience in SAP SuccessFactors. To prevent system disruptions, SAP is recommending that customers plan to adopt it by 2025 and is offering Open Office Hours to provide support.
The sessions will be held bi-weekly on Thursdays and require registration. For more details please refer to this blog in the SAP SuccessFactors Community.

 

How to Explore and Understand SAP Signavio Process Insights, Discovery Edition

SAP's popular transformation offerings, Process Discovery on Spotlight, and SAP Innovation and Optimization Pathfinder on Spotlight, will be discontinued by March 2024. Their successor, SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition, features updated content and Artificial Intelligence recommendations. You can now register for a Meet-the-Expert session to explore the results from SAP Signavio Process Insights, Discovery Edition.
Register for March 6: How to Explore and Understand SAP Signavio Process Insights, Discovery Edition

 

  • Guidance on signing up
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy Video
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy at a glance
  • Direct access to SAP Learning Hub (Login with your s-user ID and password)
  • SAP ES Academy Entitlement document
  • FAQ
  • SAP Enterprise Support value maps
  • SAP Support Portal
  • Basics: Sign up | Time zone setup |
  • Formats: EGI | MTE | AIE | Program
  • Search: Language | Keyword | Filter | Calendar
  • Upcoming live sessions in your region: APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America

entry.title: Innovate with Impact!
entry.content:
Navigating the complex landscape of innovation can be daunting. It's not just about embracing the latest trends; it's about ensuring that innovation aligns with the needs of your people, is economically viable, and technically feasible. Adding AI into the mix further complicates the equation, as the possibilities it offers are both vast and sometimes overwhelming.

You might find yourself asking, "How can I find the right balance between these factors, including the integration of AI, to create tangible value?"

While there are numerous approaches to this question, our stand-out solution is:

SAP AppHaus’ Human-Centred Approach to Innovation, coupled with its Generative AI Explore Workshop.

Why focus on human-centred design? Often, the most effective way to improve processes is to start by understanding the needs and pain points of the people who actually perform the work. While it may seem intuitive to prioritize certain aspects of innovation, it's only through engaging with end-users that you can truly identify where innovation is most needed.

But where to begin? How do other people and organizations tackle this innovation pressure? This is where thought leaders come into play—individuals who possess the expertise, insights, and innovative ideas to guide you in the right direction.

Next week, on March 13, 2024, you’ll have the opportunity to hear from Andreas Hauser, Senior Vice President and Head of the SAP AppHaus Network. With a wealth of experience in orchestrating innovation projects worldwide run by the global SAP AppHaus Network teams, Andreas collaborates closely with experts across diverse fields, gaining insights which he uses to enrich projects with customers.

Join and listen to Andreas share his invaluable insights, and actively engage in dialogue. Post your questions, share your perspectives, and glean insights from a diverse community of innovators.

By the end of the session, you'll emerge with a deeper understanding of today's opportunities, armed with strategies for leveraging SAP's Business AI with SAP Business Technology Platform, in conjunction with the SAP AppHaus Network to propel your innovation journey forward. You will get actionable insights, practical tips, and exclusive access to valuable resources that will empower you to navigate the ever-evolving landscape of innovation with confidence.

Join us here: https://www.youtube.com/live/D_QVQviszaM?si=R0un6RZCOWI-3R5M

Add to calendar: https://url.sap/uxogi7

entry.title: Partner Innovation - eMudhra's Co-Innovation Journey with SAP
entry.content:
eMudhra is a leading digital identity and transaction management company specializing in secure and efficient digital transformation solutions. At its core, eMudhra provides a wide array of solutions including digital signatures, eSignature and document workflow platform, encryption certificates, and authentication services. Among its flagship offerings, emSigner stands out as a revolutionary digital signing and workflow management tool that seamlessly integrates with enterprise platforms like SAP S/4 HANA and SuccessFactors. This integration enables businesses to digitize and automate their document-centric processes, ensuring compliance, enhancing security, and improving operational efficiency.

"We embarked on our journey with SAP's Partner Innovation Lifecycle Services (PILS) with a vision to leverage SAP's vast technological ecosystem to accelerate our innovation journey. The Co-Innovated with SAP service has been instrumental in fine-tuning emSigner to seamlessly integrate with SAP technologies, enhancing our product's capabilities and reach. This collaboration has not only expedited our development cycle but has also enriched our offerings, allowing us to deliver more value to our customers. We're grateful for this partnership, which has been a catalyst for our growth and innovation." – Kaushik Srinivasan, Co-Founder, eMudhra.

“PILS has been working with eMudhra for the past 4 years in helping them bring innovations around digital signature solutions, in compliance with local regulations.  eMudhra being a Certified Authority in India and other geographies bring a unique value with their paperless platform – “emSigner, powered by AI”, to our customers both in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP SuccessFactors.  eMudhra will continue to innovate and bring more solutions to SAP customers to embark on a paperless digital transformation.  We value our relationship with eMudhra and wish them a very successful journey with SAP and its customers” – Nandagopal B Prasad, SVP and Head, Partner Innovation Lifecycle Services, SAP.

emSigner, designed for both SAP S/4 HANA and SuccessFactors, addresses several critical business challenges:

  • Compliance and Security: Ensures that digital transactions meet global, legal and regulatory requirements, providing a secure and compliant environment for digital signatures and document management.
  • Operational Efficiency: Automates and streamlines document-centric processes, reducing manual tasks, and improving turnaround times.
  • Cost Reduction: Minimizes expenses related to paper-based processes, including printing, shipping, and storage costs.
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provides an intuitive interface for digital document management, simplifying workflows for users and improving overall satisfaction.
  • Remote Work Enablement: Facilitates secure and efficient remote document processing, critical in today’s increasingly digital and mobile workforce.

The solutions “emSigner for SAP S/4HANA Cloud” and “emSigner for SAP SuccessFactors” are available in SAP Store as well.

 

eMudhra began its journey with SAP IN 2019, focusing on integrating its digital signature and transaction management solutions with SAP’s enterprise software ecosystem. This collaboration aimed to leverage SAP's powerful platforms to enhance document security and workflow automation for SAP users globally. The partnership has been built on a shared vision of digital transformation and operational excellence.

Our experience working with SAP's Partner Innovation Lifecycle Services (PILS) on our Co-Innovation journey spans over 4 years. This engagement has been immensely valuable, providing us with insights, resources, and support needed to tailor our solutions for SAP environments. Through this partnership, we've been able to enhance our product offerings, reduce time-to-market, and expand our customer base. The collaborative approach adopted by PILS has been a cornerstone of our innovation strategy, enabling us to leverage SAP's technological prowess to meet evolving market needs.

The market outlook for emSigner solutions, tailored for SAP customers, is exceptionally positive. As businesses worldwide accelerate their digital transformation journeys, the demand for secure, compliant, and efficient digital transaction management solutions is skyrocketing. We anticipate a significant increase in adoption among SAP customers across various industries, driven by the need to enhance operational efficiency, ensure compliance, and support remote work dynamics. With our strong partnership with SAP and continuous innovation, we are well-positioned to meet this growing demand and help businesses transform their digital document management practices. emSigner’s support for remote signing and embedded signing options on SAP platforms gives its users the flexibility to address high volume use-cases and security of their native application while performing signing of documents at scale.

Furthermore, emSigner's integration with SAP provides seamless user experiences and streamlined workflows, making it easier for organizations to implement and adopt digital signing processes across their operations. This compatibility is crucial for enterprises looking to leverage their SAP investment to the fullest, ensuring that digital transformation initiatives deliver maximum value. As the global marketplace continues to evolve towards more digital and remote-friendly work environments, emSigner's solutions stand at the forefront, offering SAP customers a robust platform that not only meets today's demands but is also scalable for future needs. This positions emSigner as a pivotal solution in the digital transformation strategies of businesses aiming for growth, sustainability, and competitive advantage in the digital age.

entry.title: SAP BTP Ask Me Anything Series: SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Coming up next on the SAP BTP Ask Me Anything series: SAP Build Code. Get ready to have your questions answered live by our experts on March 27th – get all details about the session in this blog post. 

SAP Build Code was announced at SAP TechEd 2023 as THE game-changer for SAP developers. Why? Because it provides them with a seamlessly integrated environment for coding, testing, and application lifecycle management. And all that with generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, based on natural language descriptions, and optimized for Java and JavaScript.  

What's it all about?  

With SAP Build Code, you get guided experiences and prebuilt templates to quickly build applications and extend your SAP systems - watch the teaser demo. It combines the most essential services from SAP BTP and delivers a cohesive experience for developers and fusion development teams:

Fusion development is all about breaking silos between teams and empowering business and IT to collaborate successfully – and this is what combining SAP Build Code and the low-code tools in SAP Build, can help you achieve. You can learn more about how SAP Build and SAP Build Code work together in this blog post: Comprehensive Tools for Novice to Expert Developers. 

Why should I join? 

SAP Build Code is now on preview and will be generally available in March 2024!! 

This upcoming AMA is the best option for you to learn everything you need to know about the solution, including how to get access and what’s next. And, of course, you’ll have your questions answered by experts in the topic: Tim Back, Chief Development Architect for SAP BTP Application Development, Automation and Integration, Rupa Maity is a Senior Product Manager and Yuval Anafi the Lead Product Owner for SAP Build Code. 

📆 Join on March 27 – 3 pm CET – LIVE on YouTube: 

If you already have related questions, drop them in the comments below. We’ll bring them to the session, so our experts can answer them LIVE. 

See you there! 

All the best, 
Maiara  

Learn more about SAP BTP and SAP Build Code: 

SAP Build Code | Community Topic Page 

SAP BTP | Community Topic Page 

SAP BTP Innobytes: bite-sized monthly video series, sharing the latest hits and top SAP BTP innovations. 

What’s New with SAP BTP: quarterly LIVE series for SAP BTP innovation highlights, with demos and time for Q&A 

SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos 

SAP BTP Talk: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP

entry.title: Terraform Provider for SAP BTP version 1.1.0 - what’s new
entry.content:
Say hello to release 1.1.0

Sticking to the promised monthly release cycle we published version 1.1.0 of the Terraform provider for SAP BTP yesterday.  Time to check what features have been added since version 1.0.0.
